title: FIFA Last-Minute Ticket Sales Phase Opens — First-Come, First-Served for Final Seats
detail: FIFA's fourth and final official ticket sales phase — the 'Last-Minute Sales Phase' — opened at 11:00 AM ET on April 1, 2026. Tickets are sold first-come, first-served via FIFA.com/tickets and will remain available through the end of the tournament in July. Over 500 million applications were submitted in prior phases. The launch came under heavy scrutiny: dynamic pricing — a World Cup first — drew a formal letter from US Congress members calling it 'the most financially exclusionary tournament ever.' The cheapest openly available final ticket starts at $2,790, climbing to over $4,185 as availability drops. A separate PMA (Play-off Match Association) supporters phase runs simultaneously for fans of the six newly qualified teams — DR Congo, Iraq, Kosovo, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Sweden, and Denmark.

title: FIFA Officially Confirms Iran's World Cup Participation — Congress Decision Due April 30
detail: FIFA President Gianni Infantino publicly confirmed on April 1 that Iran will participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Infantino had attended Iran's 5-0 friendly win over Costa Rica in Antalya, Turkey on March 31 and afterward stated: 'Iran will be at the World Cup. We are very happy because they are a very strong team.' Iran Football Federation VP Mehdi Mohammad Nabi affirmed Iran would comply with FIFA rules and participate regardless of host country. The confirmation came despite Iran's Sports Minister Ahmad Donyamali declaring that Iran 'under no circumstances' could participate, citing the US killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei as part of ongoing US-Iran military operations. The contradiction between Iran's government and FIFA reflects the deep political tension. FIFA's next formal decision point is the FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30. Iran's Group G fixtures remain at US venues: vs New Zealand (Los Angeles, June 15), vs Belgium (Los Angeles, June 21), vs Egypt (Seattle, June 26). A US World Cup athlete exemption applies to Iranian nationals.

title: All 48 Teams Officially Confirmed — 899 Matches Over 937 Days to Fill the Field
detail: The full 48-team field for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is now officially confirmed. The qualifying campaign totaled 899 matches across 937 days, the longest and most expansive qualification process in World Cup history. The six teams confirmed on March 31 — Bosnia-Herzegovina (Group B), Kosovo (Group D), Sweden (Group F), Denmark (Group A), DR Congo (Group K), and Iraq (Group I) — complete the draw. Notable absentees from the expanded 48-team format include: Italy (third consecutive absence), Turkey, Poland, Jamaica, Bolivia, Ireland, North Macedonia, Romania, Slovakia, Czechia, New Caledonia, and Suriname. The tournament opens June 11 at Estadio Azteca and concludes July 19 at MetLife Stadium. FIFA estimates total expected viewership of approximately 6 billion across all 104 matches.
